calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-05-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Suzanne McDonnell for and with Michelle Colindres, Administrator (via Zoom).
Lisa Tandoc for Raul Colindres, Objector (via Zoom).

Ms. McDonnell stated that the accounting had been supplemented through December 31, 2024, and noted a one-cent difference in the calculation of the non-pro rata distribution to Mr. Colindres. A revised proposed order was submitted to reflect the adjustment. Ms. Tandoc objected to the revised proposed order, asserting that the distribution is in violation of the Settlement Agreement. She argued that the agreement explicitly defines net value as the appraised value minus the existing mortgage, and that the calculation submitted by Ms. McDonnell does not adhere to this definition. She contended that under the agreement, her client is entitled to 25% of the net value of the home, amounting to $250,844.28. Ms. McDonnell disagreed, stating that the petitioner has adhered to the exact language of the settlement agreement in formulating the non-pro rata distribution. She referred to a reply filed on February 6, 2025, which specifically addresses and disputes each of Ms. Tandoc's objections.

The court took the matter under submission to further review the objections and reply before issuing a ruling.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Suzanne McDonnell, via Zoom, for and with Michelle Colindres, Administrator; Lisa Tandoc, via Zoom, for and with Raul Colindres, Respondent.

The court offers its tentative ruling to take the Report of Status of Administration Petition under submission with the intent to grant and allow the administration of the estate to November of next year. No objections to the court's tentative ruling are presented. The court acknowledges the joint Case Management Statement submitted which included a request to continue the next scheduled Case Management hearing and trial for two months. The court advises that it generally does not move trial dates but after consideration, the court advises that it is inclined to grant the request. The court instructs Ms. McDonnell to submit a proposed order regarding the dates being continued to the assigned Examiner via email. The court queries counsel regarding the status of settlement discussions and whether the parties might be interested in participating in the court's pro bono mediation program if the matter qualifies to do so. Ms. McDonnell advises that the approximate value of the estate's assets is $1 million and adds that the parties have been working on a settlement agreement and that they are close and would like to continue to attempt to settle the matter amongst themselves and will consider pro bono mediation if they are unable to reach a resolution. Ms. Tandoc agrees with Ms. McDonnell's report. The court acknowledges counsels' positions and advises them to submit a formal joint request to pro bono mediation to the assigned Examiner if needed. The court reminds counsel to continue to work on Discovery so that this matter is set ready for trial if needed.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Status Petition is taken under submission. The Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar. The Case Management Hearing currently set for Feb-07-2023 is continued to Apr-04-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Trial currently set for May-02-2023 is continued to Jul-11-2023 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The Petition to Establish Estate's Claim, etc., is continued to the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Petition To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Property And For Order Cancelling Joint Tenancy Deed And Directing Transfer Of Property To Estate
Off Calendar Aug-02-2022 Continued To Nov-01-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Suzanne McDonnell, via Zoom, for and with Michelle Colindres, Petitioner; Lisa Tandoc, via Zoom, for Raul Colindres, Objector.

Ms. McDonnell reports that she believes that a framework for settlement exists, pursuant to several meet and confer sessions in July. Ms. Tandoc agrees with Ms. McDonnell and believes that additional discussions should help resolve this matter before trial. The court acknowledges the parties' efforts with this matter and points counsel to a few deficiencies in their joint case management statement (CMS) and advises counsel regarding what the court will be expecting within their next joint CMS especially if the matter has not settled by the next Case Management Hearing set for Nov-01-2022. Ms. McDonnell confirms with the court that a Petition to Approve Settlement should be filed if this matter settles before Nov-01-2022.

After additional discussion, the Petition is continued to Nov-01-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Kahler. Not Reported.

Petition To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Property And For Order Cancelling Joint Tenancy Deed And Directing Transfer Of Property To Estate
Off Calendar May-03-2022 Continued To Aug-02-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Suzanne McDonnell, via Zoom, for Michelle Colindres, via Zoom, Petitioner; Lisa Tandoc, via Zoom, for Raul Colindres, Respondent.

The court confirms that the parties have yet to meet and confer. Both Ms. McDonnell and Ms. Tandoc offer testimony regarding their respective positions in this matter. The court offers its position in this matter and urges the parties to consider participating is some form of ADR. After additional discussion regarding potential trial dates, the court advises counsel that it will be issuing a Case Management Order and offers a summary of what counsel should expect within the order. Ms. McDonnell is to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order and serve it on all parties who are entitled to notice. The parties are encouraged to meet and confer and if they agree to a mediator, submit an ex parte application for a request for mediation but only if it is being jointly requested.

The Petition is continued to Aug-02-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Andam. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Suzanne McDonnell, via ZOOM, counsel for Michelle Colindres, Petitioner.

At the outset, the court queries Ms. McDonnell regarding whether Raul Colindres has served her with his objection. Ms. McDonnell advises that she has not.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Letters of Administration is taken under submission with the intent to grant, with limited powers, and a $20,000 bond. Status on close of Estate is set for May 17, 2022 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Continued To Apr-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Suzanne McDonnell appeared by zoom for and with Michelle Colindres (Petitioner) by zoom.
Raul Colindres appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Mr. Colindres stated that he objects to the appointment of Michelle Colindres. The Court noted that Darlene and Grace declined to serve and that they nominated Michelle. Ms. McDonnell confirmed that the Court is correct. Mr. Colindres stated that he believes he has the most interest in the home and that he would be better suited to serve, that he has not filed a petition because he is the owner of the home and asked for more time to consider what he is going to do. Ms. Colindres stated that she is here to represent her Sisters and the wishes of her Parents, that they have tried to work this out amicably, that they have given her Brother a year and expressed concern stating that if the Court allows a continuance, she believes they will be here again in the same position. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to April 27, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Colindres to file a competing petition or written objections. The Court informed Mr. Colindres that he will need to be bonded and that it will appoint an Administrator at the next calling of this case.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
